segments.label: WHO Monographs — Chinese cinnamon (Cortex Cinnamomi Cassiae)
segments.text:
WHO Monograph Plant: Cortex Cinnamomi Cassiae
Common name: Chinese cinnamon
Latin name: Cinnamomum cassia
Source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, Volume 4

Write a comprehensive Plantacopia entry for this plant following WHO monograph structure. Include:
1. DEFINITION: The plant part used and its pharmaceutical definition
2. BOTANICAL DESCRIPTION: Key morphological features
3. GEOGRAPHICAL DISTRIBUTION: Native range and cultivation
4. CHEMICAL CONSTITUENTS: Major active compounds with percentages where known
5. MEDICINAL USES (Clinical evidence): Uses supported by clinical trials and systematic reviews
6. MEDICINAL USES (Traditional): Uses described in pharmacopoeias and traditional medicine
7. PHARMACOLOGY: Key mechanisms of action, in vitro and in vivo studies
8. CLINICAL EVIDENCE: Summarize 2-3 key clinical trials with outcomes
9. ADVERSE REACTIONS: Known side effects and drug interactions
10. CONTRAINDICATIONS: Specific contraindications
11. DOSAGE: WHO-recommended adult dosage

- translation: Clinical uses and pharmacological mechanism in plain English
- preparation: Dosage forms and recommended doses
- condition: Primary conditions treated (clinical evidence-based)
- source_section: WHO Monographs Vol 4 — Cortex Cinnamomi Cassiae
